signet_vault.name: Signet Vault
signet_vault.description: Encrypted local data store with key management. Implements: BIP39 mnemonic generation and recovery, SLIP-0010 Ed25519 master key derivation, HKDF-based key hierarchy (Ed25519 identity, X25519 encryption, per-device keys, Tier 3 compartment keys). Envelope encryption using aws-lc-rs (Ed25519 + X25519 + AES-256-GCM). Three-tier data model: Tier 1 (public-ish, agent-accessible), Tier 2 (sensitive, agent-accessible but conclusions-only egress enforced upstream), Tier 3 (restricted, compartment keys structurally inaccessible to agent). Content-addressed blob storage. Encrypted client-side index. Append-only hash-chained audit log (each entry includes hash of previous entry, write-path isolated from agent sessions). Per-device key provisioning via existing-device ceremony. Compile-time feature flags for SQLite+filesystem (self-hosted) vs Postgres+S3 (multi-tenant). Storage trait abstraction with two backends. User-assignable tier classification with sensible defaults. Zero secrets in memory after use.

contract.name: Signet Vault
contract.description: Encrypted local data store with key management. Implements BIP39 mnemonic generation and recovery, SLIP-0010 Ed25519 master key derivation, and a HKDF-based key hierarchy. It features a three-tier data model and an append-only hash-chained audit log.

types.name: Tier
types.description: Data tiers defining access and sensitivity levels.

types.name: AuditLogEntry

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: Unix timestamp of the audit log entry.

fields.name: previous_hash
fields.description: Hash of the previous log entry.

fields.name: current_hash
fields.description: Hash of the current log entry.

types.description: Struct representing an entry in the audit log.

types.name: Mnemonic

fields.name: phrase
fields.description: List of words constituting the mnemonic.

types.description: Struct representing a BIP39 mnemonic phrase.

types.description: Enumeration of possible errors within the Signet Vault.

functions.name: generate_mnemonic
functions.description: Generates a new BIP39 mnemonic phrase.

error_cases.name: MnemonicGenerationFailed
error_cases.condition: If the mnemonic generation process fails.

postconditions: A valid BIP39 mnemonic is generated.

functions.name: derive_key
functions.description: Derives a new key from the given mnemonic.

inputs.name: mnemonic
inputs.description: The BIP39 mnemonic to derive the key from.

inputs.name: key_type
inputs.description: The type of key to derive.

error_cases.name: KeyDerivationFailed
error_cases.condition: If the key derivation process fails.

preconditions: Input mnemonic must be a valid BIP39 mnemonic.
postconditions: A newly derived key is returned based on the key type.

functions.name: store_data
functions.description: Stores the given data in a specified tier in encrypted form.

inputs.name: data
inputs.description: The data to be encrypted and stored.

inputs.name: tier
inputs.description: The tier in which the data should be stored.

error_cases.name: EncryptionFailed
error_cases.condition: If encryption of the data fails.

error_cases.name: StorageError
error_cases.condition: If there is an issue storing the encrypted data.

postconditions: Data is encrypted and securely stored in the specified tier.

functions.name: retrieve_data
functions.description: Retrieves and decrypts data from a specified tier.

inputs.name: identifier
inputs.description: The identifier for the data to retrieve.

inputs.name: tier
inputs.description: The tier from which to retrieve the data.

error_cases.name: DecryptionFailed
error_cases.condition: If decryption of the data fails.

error_cases.name: StorageError
error_cases.condition: If the data cannot be found or there is an issue accessing storage.

preconditions: Identifier must correspond to existing stored data.
postconditions: The data is successfully decrypted and returned.

functions.name: log_action
functions.description: Logs an action into the audit log.

inputs.name: entry
inputs.description: The entry to add to the audit log.

postconditions: Action is recorded in the audit log.

invariants: Vault server never has access to plaintext
Tier 3 compartment keys are never held by the agent
All disclosures are on the audit chain
No algorithm negotiation in any protocol
Zero secrets in memory after use

signet_cred.name: Signet Credential Engine
signet_cred.description: Self-issuance credential engine. Takes structured data from vault and produces credentials in two formats: (1) SD-JWT VC per RFC 9901 using sd-jwt-rust (OpenWallet Foundation) for baseline interoperability, and (2) BBS+ signed attribute sets using anoncreds-v2-rs for unlinkable presentations. Pre-computes boolean attributes (age_over_21, balance_above_1000, etc.) in BBS+ credentials to avoid ZK circuits. Generates Pedersen commitments for numeric attributes (for downstream Bulletproof range proofs). TTL management with expiry tracking. One-time credential consumption tracking. Credentials signed by vault's identity key. Credential storage and caching within vault.

signet_proof.name: Signet Proof Workshop
signet_proof.description: Proof derivation engine operating on cached credentials. Implements: SD-JWT selective disclosure presentations, BBS+ unlinkable proofs (fresh nonce per presentation for unlinkability), Bulletproof range proofs from Pedersen commitments using dalek bulletproofs (nightly). All proofs are domain-bound (relying party origin/DID + timestamp/nonce) and time-limited. Proof composition: combine multiple proofs into a single presentation. Performance SLAs: <20ms for SD-JWT and BBS+ derivation, <200ms for Bulletproof range proofs. BBS+ unlinkability invariant must be statistically tested.

signet_policy.name: Signet Policy Engine
signet_policy.description: Policy evaluation engine. Every data request evaluated as Actor + Predicate + Context = Legitimacy. Three-way decision output: PERMIT, DENY, ANOMALY. Role hierarchy: Public < Commerce < Financial < Medical < Identity < Trusted Agent. Actor classification via four methods: explicit assignment, credential-based, domain inference, or self-declaration. ANOMALY decisions trigger escalation with structured reasoning (who, what, why unusual, options) — never silently resolved. Pattern tracker: records user approve/deny decisions per actor+predicate pair, auto-suggests policy rules based on frequency patterns (simple counter/threshold, not ML). Policies stored in vault as structured JSON. Fail-secure: pipeline timeout defaults to deny.

signet_notify.name: Signet Notification Channel
signet_notify.description: Webhook-based authorization channel for Tier 3 requests and ANOMALY escalations. Presents structured reasoning payload: who is asking, what they want, why it is unusual, what options the user has. Accepts user response: approve, deny, or modify (with scope adjustment). Timeout defaults to deny (fail-secure). Sub-second delivery target for webhooks. Implements webhook delivery with retry logic, HMAC signature verification for callback authenticity. Response propagation back to the suspended policy decision. SMS and push channels are out of scope for v1.

signet_mcp.name: Signet MCP Server
signet_mcp.description: MCP (Model Context Protocol) server bridging personal AI agent to the vault ecosystem. Authenticates personal agent via Ed25519 session keys (expiring, revocable). OAuth 2.1 with PKCE for initial agent setup/provisioning. Exposes five MCP tools: signet_get_proof (request a proof for an external service), signet_query (query vault data within tier constraints), signet_request_capability (request a PASETO v4 capability token), signet_negotiate_context (negotiate disclosure scope with external agent), signet_check_status (check pending request status). Routes every request through policy engine. Enforces tier logic: Tier 1 auto-serve proofs, Tier 2 decrypt for agent but enforce conclusions-only egress (raw data never transmitted to external services), Tier 3 suspend request and delegate to notification channel. Session management with expiry and revocation. Runs as local process or self-hosted server. All disclosures recorded to audit log.

signet_sdk.name: Signet Developer SDK
signet_sdk.description: Developer-facing SDK for external service integration. Four primitives: verify(proof, claim) — verify a Signet proof against a claimed attribute, requestCapability(spec) — request a scoped capability from a Signet MCP server, checkAuthority(signet_id) — check if a Signet identity has a given authority, parseCredential(token) — parse and validate an SD-JWT or BBS+ credential. Rust primary crate with zero transitive dependencies beyond crypto (aws-lc-rs, sd-jwt-rust, anoncreds-v2-rs). TypeScript bindings via wasm-bindgen/wasm-pack. Python bindings via PyO3. 10-line integration target for passive verification. Verification is self-contained and does not require vault access.

contract.name: Signet Developer SDK
contract.description: Developer-facing SDK for external service integration with four core primitives: verify, requestCapability, checkAuthority, and parseCredential.

types.name: Claim

fields.name: attribute
fields.description: The attributed claimed by the proof.

fields.name: value
fields.description: The value associated with the attributed claimed.

types.description: Represents a claim consisting of an attribute and its associated value.

types.name: CapabilitySpec

fields.name: expiration
fields.description: Expiration time of the capability in Unix timestamp.

types.description: Specifies a capability by describing its permissions and expiration.

types.name: SignetId

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the Signet identity.

types.description: Represents a unique identifier for a Signet identity.

types.name: SignetError
types.description: Possible errors that may occur during SDK operations.

functions.name: verify
functions.description: Verifies a Signet proof against a claimed attribute.

error_cases.name: InvalidProof
error_cases.condition: Proof data format is invalid or corrupted.

error_cases.name: InvalidClaim
error_cases.condition: The claim does not match the verified proof.

postconditions: Returns true if the proof is valid for the claim, otherwise false.

functions.name: requestCapability
functions.description: Requests a scoped capability from a Signet MCP server.

error_cases.name: CapabilityRequestFailed
error_cases.condition: Request to MCP server failed due to network issues or invalid spec.

postconditions: Returns a capability token if request is successful.

functions.name: checkAuthority
functions.description: Checks whether a Signet identity possesses a specified authority.

error_cases.name: AuthorityCheckFailed
error_cases.condition: The authority check could not be completed.

postconditions: Returns true if the identity has authority, otherwise false.

functions.name: parseCredential
functions.description: Parses and validates an SD-JWT or BBS+ credential.

error_cases.name: CredentialParseError
error_cases.condition: Token is not valid or cannot be parsed.

preconditions: Token should be a valid SD-JWT or BBS+ format.
postconditions: Returns a claim if token is successfully parsed and validated.

invariants: All operations must return consistent results given the same input.
Tokens and metadata must remain confidential and not be stored in plaintext.

root.name: Root
root.description:
# Signet: Personal Sovereign Agent Stack

Build a personal data sovereignty system where the user's encrypted vault is the cryptographic root of trust, a personal agent acts as steward carrying proofs

contract.name: Root

types.name: RootError
types.description: Comprehensive error type for the Root component aggregating errors from all dependencies.

types.name: RootConfig

fields.name: policy_engine_config
fields.description: Configuration settings for the policy engine.

types.description: Configuration settings for the Root component.

functions.name: initialize_root
functions.description: Initializes the root component with provided configuration.
preconditions: Config must be a valid RootConfig instance.
postconditions: The Root component is ready to handle requests.

functions.name: handle_request
functions.description: Processes a request by routing it through various components.

error_cases.name: invalid_request
error_cases.condition: The request does not conform to the expected schema.

preconditions: Server is running and ready to accept requests.
postconditions: A JsonRpcResponse is generated for the request.

functions.name: shutdown_root
functions.description: Gracefully shuts down the root component.

error_cases.name: shutdown_failed
error_cases.condition: One of the dependent components fails to shut down properly.

postconditions: All components are shut down and system resources are released.

invariants: Vault server never has access to plaintext.
All disclosures are on the audit chain.
No algorithm negotiation in any protocol.
Zero secrets in memory after use.
